message:
type: PROPOSE

note: I am implementing deep-merge for Features.update() in src/datasets/features/features.py. Please let me know your files so we can keep them disjoint.

type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/datasets/features/features.py
note: My task: add keep_features_dicts_synced decorator to Features class to sync _column_requires_decoding dict. Only touches features.py. What file(s) does your feature need? If it also needs features.py, let's discuss splitting by function/region.

type: REVISE
my_files: src/datasets/features/features.py (Features.update method body only)
your_files: src/datasets/features/features.py (decorator definition + applying it to all other mutating methods, e.g. __setitem__, __delitem__, etc.)
note: We both touch features.py but can split by region: I will ONLY change the body of Features.update() to add deep-merge logic. Please define your keep_features_dicts_synced decorator elsewhere in the file and apply it to other mutating methods, but do NOT edit update()'s body. If you also want update() decorated, tell me the exact decorator name/signature and I'll add '@keep_features_dicts_synced' above update() myself so we don't collide. Sound good?

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/datasets/features/features.py
your_files: src/datasets/features/features.py
note: We both need features.py (shared file), no other files needed for either feature. To minimize merge conflicts: I'll add a keep_features_dicts_synced decorator and apply it (as a single @decorator line placed directly above each def) to __setitem__, __delitem__, update, and possibly pop/popitem/setdefault/clear -- I will NOT touch the internal body/logic of update(), only add the decorator line above 'def update(...)'. Please keep your deep-merge logic entirely inside the body of update() (the for-loops / merge logic), not the signature line, so our diffs land on different lines and merge cleanly. I'll resolve any residual conflicts manually when I merge team/agent2 at the end per the git collaboration protocol. Sending DONE when finished.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/datasets/features/features.py (body of update() only)
your_files: src/datasets/features/features.py (decorator definition + decorator lines above mutating methods, not touching update() body)
note: Agreed. I will only touch the internal body/logic inside def update(...), not its signature line. Proceeding to implement now.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/datasets/features/features.py (keep_features_dicts_synced decorator definition + applying it to __setitem__, __delitem__, and other mutating methods except update's body)
your_files: src/datasets/features/features.py (Features.update method body only)
note: Agreed. Decorator: 'def keep_features_dicts_synced(func): ... @wraps(func) def wrapper(self, *args, **kwargs): out = func(self, *args, **kwargs); self._column_requires_decoding = {col: require_decoding(feature) for col, feature in self.items()}; return out ... return wrapper' defined near the top of features.py module level (or just above class Features). Please add exactly '@keep_features_dicts_synced' as the decorator line directly above 'def update(self, *args, **kwargs):' yourself, and only change the body/logic inside update() for deep-merge. I will not touch update() at all (no signature, no body, no decorator line) -- that's fully yours. I'll apply the decorator to __setitem__, __delitem__, pop, popitem, setdefault, clear, __ior__ etc. Will send DONE when finished.
